volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Hippolytus (Section 313 - Section 425)

sections.label: Section 313
sections.original: ὁρᾷς ; φρονεῖς μὲν εὖ , φρονοῦσα δ ’ οὐ θέλεις
sections.translation: There now ! thou art thyself again , but e ’ en yet refusest to aid thy children and preserve thy life .

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: φιλῶ τέκν ’· ἄλλῃ δ ’ ἐν τύχῃ χειμάζομαι .
sections.translation: My babes I love , but there is another storm that buffets me .

sections.label: Section 316
sections.original: ἁγνὰς μέν , ὦ παῖ , χεῖρας αἵματος φορεῖς ;
sections.translation: Daughter , are thy hands from bloodshed pure ?

sections.label: Section 317
sections.original: χεῖρες μὲν ἁγναί , φρὴν δ ’ ἔχει μίασμά τι .
sections.translation: My hands are pure , but on my soul there rests a stain .

sections.label: Section 318
sections.original: μῶν ἐξ ἐπακτοῦ πημονῆς ἐχθρῶν τινος ;
sections.translation: The issue of some enemy ’ s secret witchery ?

sections.label: Section 319
sections.original: φίλος μ ’ ἀπόλλυσ ’ οὐχ ἑκοῦσαν οὐχ ἑκών .
sections.translation: A friend is my destroyer , one unwilling as myself .

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: Θησεύς τιν ’ ἡμάρτηκεν ἐς σ ’ ἁμαρτίαν ;
sections.translation: Hath Theseus wronged thee in any wise ?

sections.label: Section 321
sections.original: μὴ δρῶσ ’ ἔγωγ ’ ἐκεῖνον ὀφθείην κακῶς .
sections.translation: Never may I prove untrue to him [*]

sections.label: Section 322
sections.original: τί γὰρ τὸ δεινὸν τοῦθ ’ ὅ σ ’ ἐξαίρει θανεῖν ;
sections.translation: Then what strange mystery is there that drives thee on to die ?

sections.label: Section 323
sections.original: ἔα μ ’ ἁμαρτεῖν · οὐ γὰρ ἐς σὲ ἁμαρτάνω .
sections.translation: O , let my sin and me alone ! ’ tis not ’ gainst thee I sin .

sections.label: Section 324
sections.original: οὐ δῆθ ’ ἑκοῦσά γ ’, ἐν δὲ σοὶ λελείψομαι .
sections.translation: Never willingly ! and , if I fail , ’ twill rest at thy door .

sections.label: Section 325
sections.original: τί δρᾷς ; βιάζῃ χειρὸς ἐξαρτωμένη ;
sections.translation: How now ? thou usest force in clinging to my hand .

sections.label: Section 326
sections.original: καὶ σῶν γε γονάτων , κοὐ μεθήσομαί ποτε .
sections.translation: Yea , and I will never loose my hold upon thy knees .

sections.label: Section 327
sections.original: κάκ ’, ὦ τάλαινα , σοὶ τάδ ’, εἰ πεύσῃ , κακά .
sections.translation: Alas for thee ! my sorrows , shouldst thou learn them , would recoil on thee .

sections.label: Section 328
sections.original: μεῖζον γὰρ ἢ σοῦ μὴ τυχεῖν τί μοι κακόν ;
sections.translation: What keener grief for me than failing to win thee ?

sections.label: Section 329
sections.original: ὀλεῖς · τὸ μέντοι πρᾶγμ ’ ἐμοὶ τιμὴν φέρει .
sections.translation: ’ Twill be death to thee ; though to me that brings renown . [*]

sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: κἄπειτα κρύπτεις χρήσθ ’ ἱκνουμένης ἐμοῦ ;
sections.translation: And dost thou then conceal this boon despite my prayers ?

sections.label: Section 331
sections.original: ἐκ τῶν γὰρ αἰσχρῶν ἐσθλὰ μηχανώμεθα .
sections.translation: I do , for ’ tis out of shame I am planning an honourable escape .

sections.label: Section 332
sections.original: οὐκοῦν λέγουσα τιμιωτέρα φανῇ .
sections.translation: Tell it , and thine honour shall the brighter shine .

sections.label: Section 333
sections.original: ἄπελθε πρὸς θεῶν δεξιᾶς τ ’ ἐμῆς μέθες .
sections.translation: Away , I do conjure thee ; loose my hand .

sections.label: Section 334
sections.original: οὐ δῆτ ’, ἐπεί μοι δῶρον οὐ δίδως ὃ χρῆν .
sections.translation: I will not , for the boon thou shouldst have granted me is denied .

sections.label: Section 335
sections.original: δώσω · σέβας γὰρ χειρὸς αἰδοῦμαι τὸ σόν .
sections.translation: I will grant it out of reverence for thy holy sup- pliant touch .

sections.label: Section 336
sections.original: σιγῷμ ’ ἂν ἤδη · σὸς γὰρ οὑντεῦθεν λόγος .
sections.translation: Henceforth I hold my peace ; ’ tis thine to speak from now .

sections.label: Section 337
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον , οἷον , μῆτερ , ἠράσθης ἔρον ,
sections.translation: Ah ! hapless mother , [*] what a love was thine !

sections.label: Section 338
sections.original: ὃν ἔσχε Ταύρου , τέκνον , ἢ τί φῂς τόδε ;
sections.translation: Her love for the bull ? daughter , or what meanest thou ?

sections.label: Section 339
sections.original: σύ τ ’, ὦ τάλαιν ’ ὅμαιμε , Διονύσου δάμαρ ,
sections.translation: And woe to thee ! my sister , [*] bride of Dionysus .

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: τέκνον , τί πάσχεις ; συγγόνους κακορροθεῖς ;
sections.translation: What ails thee , child ? speaking ill of kith and kin .

sections.label: Section 341
sections.original: τρίτη δ ’ ἐγὼ δύστηνος ὡς ἀπόλλυμαι .
sections.translation: Myself the third to suffer ! how am I undone !

sections.label: Section 342
sections.original: ἔκ τοι πέπληγμαι · ποῖ προβήσεται λόγος ;
sections.translation: Thou strik ’ st me dumb ! Where will this history end ?

sections.label: Section 343
sections.original: ἐκεῖθεν ἡμεῖς , οὐ νεωστί , δυστυχεῖς .
sections.translation: That love has been our curse from time long past .

sections.label: Section 344
sections.original: οὐδέν τι μᾶλλον οἶδ ’ ἃ βούλομαι κλύειν .
sections.translation: I know no more of what I fain would learn .

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! would thou couldst say for me what I have to tell .

sections.label: Section 346
sections.original: οὐ μάντις εἰμὶ τἀφανῆ γνῶναι σαφῶς .
sections.translation: I am no prophetess to unriddle secrets .

sections.label: Section 347
sections.original: τί τοῦθ ’, ὃ δὴ λέγουσιν ἀνθρώπους , ἐρᾶν ;
sections.translation: What is it they mean when they talk of people being in love ?

sections.label: Section 348
sections.original: ἥδιστον , ὦ παῖ , ταὐτὸν ἀλγεινόν θ ’ ἅμα .
sections.translation: At once the sweetest and the bitterest thing , my child .

sections.label: Section 349
sections.original: ἡμεῖς ἂν εἶμεν θατέρῳ κεχρημένοι .
sections.translation: I shall only find the latter half .

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: τί φῄς ; ἐρᾷς , ὦ τέκνον ; ἀνθρώπων τίνος ;
sections.translation: Ha ! my child , art thou in love ?

sections.label: Section 351
sections.original: ὅστις ποθ ’ οὗτός ἐσθ ’, ὁ τῆς Ἀμαζόνος ⋯
sections.translation: The Amazon ’ s son , whoever he may be ,—

sections.label: Section 352
sections.original: Ἱππόλυτον αὐδᾷς ;
sections.translation: Mean ’ st thou Hippolytus ?

sections.label: Section 352b
sections.original: σοῦ τάδ ’, οὐκ ἐμοῦ κλύεις .
sections.translation: ’ Twas thou , not I , that spoke his name .

sections.label: Section 353
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί λέξεις , τέκνον ; ὥς μ ’ ἀπώλεσας .
sections.translation: O heavens ! what is this , my child ? Thou hast ruined me . Outrageous ! friends ; I will not live and bear it ;

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: ζῶσ ’· ἐχθρὸν ἦμαρ , ἐχθρὸν εἰσορῶ φάος .
sections.translation: hateful is life , hateful to mine eyes the light . This body I resign , will cast it off , and rid me of existence by my death . Farewell , my life is o ’ er . Yea , for the chaste have wicked passions , ’ gainst their will maybe , but still they have . Cypris , it seems , is not a goddess after all ,

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ εἴ τι μεῖζον ἄλλο γίγνεται θεοῦ ,
sections.translation: but something greater far , for she hath been the ruin of my lady and of me and our whole family .

sections.label: Section 1.362
sections.original: ἄιες ὤ , ἔκλυες ὤ ,
sections.translation: O , too clearly didst thou hear our queen uplift her voice to tell her startling tale of piteous suffering . Come death ere I reach thy state of feeling , [*] loved mistress .

sections.label: Section 1.365
sections.original: κατανύσαι φρενῶν . ἰώ μοι , φεῦ φεῦ .
sections.translation: O horrible ! woe , for these miseries ! woe , for the sorrows on which mortals feed ! Thou art undone ! thou hast disclosed thy sin to heaven ’ s light . What hath each passing day and every hour in store for thee ?

sections.label: Section 1.370
sections.original: — τελευτάσεταί τι καινὸν δόμοις .
sections.translation: Some strange event will come to pass in this house . For it is no longer uncertain where the star of thy love is setting , thou hapless daughter [*] of Crete .

sections.label: Section 373
sections.original: Τροζήνιαι γυναῖκες , αἳ τόδ ’ ἔσχατον
sections.translation: Ladies of Troezen , who dwell here upon the frontier edge of Pelops ’ land ,

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: ἤδη ποτ ’ ἄλλως νυκτὸς ἐν μακρῷ χρόνῳ
sections.translation: oft ere now in heedless mood through the long hours of night have I wondered why man ’ s life is spoiled ; and it seems to me their evil case is not due to any natural fault of judgment , for there be many dowered with sense , but we must view the matter in this light ;

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: τὰ χρήστ ’ ἐπιστάμεσθα καὶ γιγνώσκομεν ,
sections.translation: by teaching and experience we learn the right but neglect it in practice , some from sloth , others from preferring pleasure of some kind or other to duty . Now life has many pleasures , protracted talk , and leisure , that seductive evil ;

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: αἰδώς τε . δισσαὶ δ ’ εἰσίν , ἣ μὲν οὐ κακή ,
sections.translation: likewise there is shame which is of two kinds , one a noble quality , the other a curse to families ; but if for each its proper time were clearly known , these twain could not have had the selfsame letters to denote them .

sections.label: Section 388
sections.original: ταῦτ ’ οὖν ἐπειδὴ τυγχάνω φρονοῦσ ’ ἐγώ ,
sections.translation: So then since I had made up my mind on these points , ’ twas not likely any drug would alter it

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: ἔμελλον , ὥστε τοὔμπαλιν πεσεῖν φρενῶν .
sections.translation: and make me think the contrary . And I will tell thee too the way my judgment went . When love wounded me , I bethought me how I best might bear the smart . So from that day forth I began to hide in silence what I suffered .

sections.label: Section 395
sections.original: γλώσσῃ γὰρ οὐδὲν πιστόν , ἣ θυραῖα μὲν
sections.translation: For I put no faith in counsellors , who know well to lecture others for presumption , yet themselves have countless troubles of their own . Next I did devise noble endurance of these wanton thoughts , striving by continence for victory .

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: τρίτον δ ’, ἐπειδὴ τοισίδ ’ οὐκ ἐξήνυτον
sections.translation: And last when I could not succeed in mastering love hereby , methought it best to die ; and none can gainsay my purpose . For fain I would my virtue should to all appear , my shame have few to witness it .

sections.label: Section 405
sections.original: τὸ δ ’ ἔργον ᾔδη τὴν νόσον τε δυσκλεᾶ ,
sections.translation: I knew my sickly passion now ; to yield to it I saw how infamous ; and more , I learnt to know so well that I was but a woman , a thing the world detests . Curses , hideous curses on that wife , who first did shame her marriage-vow for lovers other than her lord ! ’ Twas from noble families

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: τόδ ’ ἦρξε θηλείαισι γίγνεσθαι κακόν .
sections.translation: this curse began to spread among our sex . For when the noble countenance disgrace , poor folk of course will think that it is right . Those too I hate who make profession of purity , though in secret reckless sinners .

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original: αἳ πῶς ποτ ’, ὦ δέσποινα ποντία Κύπρι ,
sections.translation: How can these , queen Cypris , ocean ’ s child , e ’ er look their husbands in the face ? do they never feel one guilty thrill that their accomplice , night , or the chambers of their house will find a voice and speak ?

sections.label: Section 419
sections.original: ἡμᾶς γὰρ αὐτὸ τοῦτ ’ ἀποκτείνει , φίλαι ,
sections.translation: This it is that calls on me to die , kind friends ,

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original: ὡς μήποτ ’ ἄνδρα τὸν ἐμὸν αἰσχύνασ ’ ἁλῶ ,
sections.translation: that so I may ne ’ er be found to have disgraced my lord , or the children I have born ; no ! may they grow up and dwell in glorious Athens , free to speak and act , heirs to such fair fame as a mother can bequeath . For to know that father or mother have sinned doth turn

sections.label: Section 425
sections.original: ὅταν ξυνειδῇ μητρὸς ἢ πατρὸς κακά .
sections.translation: the stoutest heart to slavishness . This alone , men say , can stand the buffets of life ’ s battle , a just and virtuous soul in whomsoever found . For time unmasks the villain sooner or later , holding up to them a mirror as to some blooming maid .
